summary: A superb Hunter‑Seamons matrix mix captures a high‑energy Hartford show highlighted by soaring Space, Black Peter and a tight first set.
review: The performance is praised for its inspired playing – especially the wild Phil “earthquake” space, a powerful Black Peter and a flawless first‑set flow that builds into an ecstatic second set.  The matrix recording is described as crystal‑clear with superb keyboard texture, though a few listeners note it sits a touch farther back than Seamons’ very best mixes.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are highly favorable (4‑5 stars) and emphasize both an excellent musical show and a high‑quality matrix recording; the consistency across reviewers yields strong confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Keyboard texture is praised as great, overall sound described as incredible and crisp; one reviewer notes it feels slightly more distant than Seamons’ top‑tier mixes but still very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is called a ‘great’ and tightly‑arranged opener; second set shines with the Phil “earthquake” space and a seamless transition from Playing in the Band into Eyes, keeping the momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is noted as strong and mellowed by years of singing.
band_member_comments.Phil: The ‘earthquake’ spoken‑space segment is highlighted as a wild, memorable moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard texture is described as great; Brent Mydland sits perfectly in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Listeners hear every drum nuance and describe the drumming as part of the layered perfection.
